change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: The project is pursuing a multi-level approach to develop relevant quality infrastructure services for businesses. It provides training to the partners’ specialist and management staff in order to strengthen the capacities of organizations as a whole and enable them to be more easily integrated into international networks. The main partner is the Nepal Bureau of Standards and Metrology (NBSM). Further public partners are the Department of Food Technology and Quality Control (DFTQC) and the Department of Plant Resources (DPR). Additionally, the project cooperates with business associations from the pharmaceutical and dairy sectors. A total of three interrelated outputs are targeted: (1) Strengthening the national metrology and QI systems;  (2) Improving the analytical capabilities of the Nepali food authority; (3) Quality assurance in the pharmaceutical sector and in the dairy industry. The project’s objectives are achieved through metrological and analytical lab training, qualifying auditors for specific certifications and accreditations, providing close consultation, and conducting mock audits. Furthermore, the partners are supported through the procurement of selected instruments.
